ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken wing food product entry; boneless battered variant is not separately coded. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific boneless battered chicken wing code. A00GJ (cooked chicken meat) is the closest defensible parent node.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for boneless battered chicken wing. Null used to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single boneless chicken wing piece weighs approximately 45 g, equivalent to one piece as commonly served in fast-food or hawker settings in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Boneless Chicken Wing (Original) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Boneless Chicken Wing (Original), cr_id F11020010. All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this record at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for boneless battered deep-fried chicken wing. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous battered and fried chicken products from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. The batter coating contributes refined carbohydrate (wheat flour) with a moderate GI, but the high protein and fat content of the chicken substantially blunts the glycaemic response, supporting a low GI estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 10.9 × 0.45) / 100 = 2.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 361 mg per 100 g; deep-fried preparation with saturated fat contributing to arterial stiffness
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this food; sodium at 361 mg/100 g approaches one-sixth of the 2,000 mg daily limit in a single 45 g serving, and the saturated fat content further elevates cardiovascular risk; choose baked or grilled chicken preparations and avoid high-sodium dipping sauces.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Batter coating adds refined carbohydrate (10.9 g/100 g); high protein (21.5 g) and fat (18.19 g) moderate glycaemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes may consume this food in small portions occasionally; the low estimated GI (~45) and high protein content moderate postprandial glucose rise, but the refined wheat batter and deep-frying method are suboptimal; prefer grilled or baked skinless chicken and avoid sweet dipping sauces that add rapidly absorbed sugars.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~4.5 g/100 g), trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g), high sodium (361 mg/100 g), and deep-frying-generated advanced glycation end-products (AGEs)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid or strictly limit this food; the combination of saturated fat, trans fat, high sodium, and AGEs from deep-frying collectively elevates LDL cholesterol, promotes endothelial dysfunction, and increases blood pressure, all of which worsen cardiovascular outcomes; substitute with baked or grilled skinless chicken breast.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates AGEs that promote joint inflammation; trans fat and saturated fat stimulate pro-inflammatory cytokine activity
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried foods including this product; AGEs formed during deep-frying, combined with trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g) and saturated fat (~4.5 g/100 g), exacerbate synovial inflammation and joint damage; choose steamed, poached, or baked chicken preparations instead.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Boneless Chicken Wing (Original) is a deep-fried chicken product made from deboned chicken wing meat coated in a seasoned wheat flour batter and cooked in hot oil until golden and crispy. The exterior is crunchy and lightly salted, while the interior remains juicy and tender. This product is widely sold in fast-food chains and hawker stalls across Singapore and is commonly consumed as a snack, side dish, or part of a set meal. Per 100 g, it provides 293 kcal, 21.5 g protein, 18.19 g fat (including approximately 4.5 g saturated fat and 0.1 g trans fat), and 10.9 g carbohydrate from the batter coating, with 361 mg sodium. The high sodium and saturated fat content make it unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The deep-frying process generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs), which are pro-inflammatory and of concern for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis. Despite the fried coating, the glycaemic index is estimated to be low (~45) due to the high protein and fat content blunting postprandial glucose response, making it moderately acceptable for individuals with type 2 diabetes in small portions. Nutritional data sourced from the Singapore HPB Foodo database (cr_id F11020010).
common_user_queries: How many calories in a boneless chicken wing?
Can diabetics eat boneless chicken wings?
How much protein in boneless chicken wing?
Is boneless chicken wing high in sodium?
Boneless chicken wing calories per piece
Is boneless chicken wing suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the Singapore HPB Foodo database (cr_id F11020010) at confidence 0.95. This is an exact match from a primary approved regional source. Saturated fat and trans fat values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous fried chicken products and documented accordingly. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021, which is the best available approved GI evidence for this food type.
notes: sugar_g is set to -1: the HPB source records sugar as -1.0 (data not available) for this product; detailed sugar breakdown is not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for F11020010. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for this specific product are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central for an exact boneless battered chicken wing match as of 2026-04-16. potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this product. saturated_fat_g (~4.5 g/100 g) and trans_fat_g (~0.1 g/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for fried battered chicken (USDA FDC ID 171477, Chicken, wing, frozen, glazed, reheated) as the closest approved-source analogue; these are approximate values. The input dietary_tag 'wholegrain' has been removed as it is not applicable to a deep-fried battered chicken product. The occasion_tag 'breakfast' from the input has been retained in meal_context as 'snack' and 'fast food meal' which are more accurate; breakfast is not a primary context for this food type.
